1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To God's chosen people who are living as strangers, scattered throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.

God the Father chose you, just as he planned long ago. The Spirit has made you holy, so that you would obey Jesus Christ and be made clean by his blood. May God give you more and more grace and peace.
Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from death.
He has given us an inheritance that can never spoil, be ruined, or fade away. It is kept in heaven for you,
who are guarded by God's power through faith, until the salvation that is ready to be shown at the last time.
So you are full of joy, even though right now, for a little while, you may have to suffer many kinds of trouble.

These troubles come to prove that your faith is real. Your faith is worth far more than gold, which is tested by fire even though it does not last. When your faith is proven, it will bring praise, glory, and honor when Jesus Christ appears.
You have not seen Christ, yet you love him. You do not see him now, yet you believe in him, and you are filled with a joy too great for words, a glorious joy,
because you are receiving the goal of your faith: the salvation of your souls.
The prophets searched carefully into this salvation. They spoke about the grace that was coming to you.
They tried to find out the time and situation the Spirit of Christ in them was pointing to, when he told ahead of time about the sufferings of Christ and the glory that would follow.

It was shown to them that they were not serving themselves, but you, when they spoke of the things that have now been told to you. These things were told by those who preached the good news to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven. Even the angels long to look into these things.
So get your minds ready for action. Keep clear heads. Set your hope fully on the grace that will be given to you when Jesus Christ appears.
As obedient children, do not shape your lives by the wrong desires you had when you did not know any better.
Instead, be holy in everything you do, just as the One who called you is holy.
For the Scripture says: Be holy, because I am holy.
You call on God as Father, and he judges each person's work fairly, without favoring anyone. So live with deep respect for God during your time here as strangers.
For you know that you were set free from the empty way of life you learned from your ancestors. You were not set free with things that do not last, like silver or gold.
You were set free by the precious blood of Christ, a lamb with no fault or flaw.
Christ was chosen before the world was made, but he was shown to the world in these last times, for your sake.
Through Christ you believe in God, who raised him from death and gave him glory. So your faith and hope are in God.
You have made yourselves clean by obeying the truth, so that you truly love your brothers and sisters. So love one another deeply, from a pure heart.
You have been born again — not from seed that dies, but from seed that lasts forever, through the living and lasting word of God.
For the Scripture says: All people are like grass, and all their glory is like the flowers of the field. The grass dries up and the flowers fall,
but the word of the Lord lasts forever. And this word is the good news that was preached to you.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So get rid of all hate, all lying, all pretending, all jealousy, and all evil words.
Like newborn babies, long for the pure milk of God's word, so that by it you may grow up into salvation,
now that you have tasted that the Lord is good.
Come to Christ, the living stone. People rejected him, but God chose him and treasures him.
And you also, like living stones, are being built into a spiritual house. You are a holy group of priests, offering spiritual sacrifices that please God through Jesus Christ.
For the Scripture says: Look, I am laying a stone in Zion, a chosen and precious cornerstone. The one who trusts in him will never be put to shame.
So to you who believe, this stone is precious. But to those who do not believe: The stone the builders threw away has become the most important stone,
and: A stone that makes people trip, a rock that makes them fall. They trip because they do not obey the message — which is what was bound to happen to them.

But you are a chosen people, a royal group of priests, a holy nation, a people who belong to God. He chose you so that you could tell others about the wonderful things he has done — the One who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
Once you were not a people, but now you are the people of God. Once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
Dear friends, you are like strangers and travelers in this world. So I beg you to stay away from sinful desires, which fight against your soul.
Live good lives among those who do not believe. Then, even if they accuse you of doing wrong, they will see your good deeds and give glory to God on the day he comes.
For the Lord's sake, obey every human authority: whether the king, as the highest authority,
or the governors, who are sent by him to punish those who do wrong and to praise those who do right.
For it is God's will that by doing good you will silence the foolish talk of people who do not understand.
Live as free people, but do not use your freedom as a cover-up for doing evil. Live as servants of God.
Show proper respect to everyone. Love your fellow believers. Have deep respect for God. Honor the king.
Servants, obey your masters with full respect — not only those who are good and gentle, but also those who are harsh.
For it deserves praise when someone, because of his awareness of God, puts up with the pain of suffering unfairly.
But there is no credit in taking a beating for doing wrong. Yet if you suffer for doing good and you put up with it, this pleases God.
This is what you were called to do, because Christ suffered for you, leaving you an example to follow in his steps.
He never sinned, and no lie was ever found in his mouth.
When people insulted him, he did not insult them back. When he suffered, he did not threaten. Instead, he trusted himself to God, who judges fairly.
Christ himself carried our sins in his own body on the cross, so that we might die to sin and live for what is right. By his wounds you have been healed.
For you were like sheep going the wrong way, but now you have come back to the Shepherd who watches over your souls.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: In the same way, wives, follow the lead of your husbands. Then, if any of them do not obey the word, they may be won over without a word, by the way their wives live,
when they see your pure lives lived with respect.
Your beauty should not come from the outside — from fancy hairstyles, gold jewelry, or fine clothes.
Instead, it should come from inside, the beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit that does not fade. This is very precious to God.
This is how the holy women of long ago made themselves beautiful. They put their hope in God and followed the lead of their husbands.
Sarah was like this: she obeyed Abraham and called him her lord. You are her daughters if you do what is right and do not give in to fear.

In the same way, husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way. Treat your wife with honor, as the one who is more delicate and as an equal heir of the gift of life, so that nothing will get in the way of your prayers.
Finally, all of you, live in harmony. Feel for one another, love one another as family, be kind, and be humble.
Do not pay back evil with evil, or insult with insult. Instead, pay back with a blessing, because you were called to receive a blessing.
For the Scripture says: Whoever wants to love life and see good days must keep his tongue from evil and his lips from telling lies.
He must turn away from evil and do good. He must look for peace and go after it.
For the Lord watches over those who do right, and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord is against those who do evil.
Who is going to harm you if you are eager to do good?
But even if you do suffer for doing right, you are blessed. Do not be afraid of what they fear, and do not be troubled.
Instead, in your hearts honor Christ as Lord. Always be ready to give an answer to anyone who asks you the reason for the hope you have. But do it with gentleness and respect,
keeping a clear conscience, so that those who speak evil of your good life in Christ will be ashamed of their lies.
It is better to suffer for doing good, if that is what God wants, than for doing evil.
For Christ also suffered once for sins — the one who was right, for those who were wrong — to bring you to God. He was put to death in his body, but made alive in the Spirit.
In the Spirit he also went and preached to the spirits in prison,
who had disobeyed long ago, when God waited patiently in the days of Noah while the ark was being built. In the ark only a few people — eight in all — were saved through water.

This water is a picture of baptism, which now saves you too. Baptism is not about washing dirt off the body. It is the promise of a clean conscience toward God. It saves you through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
who has gone into heaven and is at God's right hand, with angels, authorities, and powers under his control.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So, since Christ suffered in his body, arm yourselves with the same way of thinking. For whoever has suffered in the body is finished with sin.
As a result, he does not live the rest of his life chasing human desires, but doing what God wants.
You have already spent enough time in the past doing what people who do not know God like to do. You lived in wild living, evil desires, drunkenness, wild parties, and disgusting worship of idols.
They think it is strange that you no longer join them in their wild, out-of-control living, so they say evil things about you.
But they will have to explain themselves to God, who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
This is why the good news was preached even to those who are now dead: so that, although they were judged in the body as all people are, they might live in the spirit as God lives.
The end of all things is near. So keep clear minds and be alert, so that you can pray.
Most important of all, love each other deeply, because love covers over many sins.
Welcome one another into your homes without complaining.
Each of you has received a gift from God. So use it to serve one another, as faithful managers of God's grace, which comes in many forms.

If anyone speaks, he should speak as one giving the very words of God. If anyone serves, he should serve with the strength God gives. Then in everything God will be praised through Jesus Christ. To him be glory and power forever and ever. Amen.
Dear friends, do not be surprised at the painful trials you are going through, as if something strange were happening to you.
Instead, be glad that you share in the sufferings of Christ, so that you will be filled with joy when his glory is shown.
If people insult you because of the name of Christ, you are blessed, because the glorious Spirit of God rests on you.
None of you should suffer for being a murderer, a thief, an evildoer, or someone who meddles in other people's business.
But if you suffer for being a Christian, do not be ashamed. Instead, praise God that you bear that name.
For the time has come for judgment to begin with God's own family. And if it begins with us, what will happen to those who do not obey the good news of God?
And: If it is hard even for those who are right with God to be saved, what will happen to the sinful and the godless?
So those who suffer because it is God's will should keep on doing good and trust their souls to their faithful Creator.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Now I have a word for the elders among you. I am an elder too, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and I will also share in the glory that is going to be shown.
Take care of God's flock that is under your care. Watch over them — not because you have to, but because you want to, as God wants. Do it not for dishonest money, but because you are eager to serve.
Do not act like bosses over the people in your care. Instead, be examples to the flock.
And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive a crown of glory that will never fade.
In the same way, you younger ones, follow the lead of those who are older. And all of you, treat one another with humility, because God is against the proud, but gives grace to the humble.
So make yourselves low under God's mighty hand, and at the right time he will lift you up.
Give all your worries to him, because he cares for you.
Keep clear minds and stay alert. Your enemy the devil is on the move like a roaring lion, looking for someone to swallow up.
Stand against him and stay strong in the faith. Remember that your brothers and sisters all over the world are going through the same kinds of suffering.
And the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory in Christ, will himself make you strong, firm, and steady after you have suffered for a little while.
To him be the power forever and ever. Amen.
With the help of Silas, whom I consider a faithful brother, I have written this short letter to encourage you and to tell you that this is the true grace of God. Stand firm in it.
The church in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you greetings, and so does my son Mark.
Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.
